heading: Emergency actions
label: 6592c Emergency actions
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act, and not as part of the Healthy Forests Restoration Act of 2003 which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Authorized emergency action
children.label: 1 Authorized emergency action
children.text: The term “authorized emergency action” means an action carried out pursuant to an emergency situation determination issued under this section to mitigate the harm to life, property, or important natural or cultural resources on National Forest System land or adjacent land.

children.label: A
children.text: Relief from hazards threatening human health and safety.

children.label: B
children.text: Mitigation of threats to natural resources on National Forest System land or adjacent land.

children.heading: Emergency situation determination
children.label: 3 Emergency situation determination
children.text: The term “emergency situation determination” means a determination made by the Secretary under subsection (b)(1)(A).

children.heading: Land and resource management plan
children.label: 4 Land and resource management plan
children.text: The term “land and resource management plan” means a plan developed under .section 1604 of this title

children.heading: National Forest System land
children.label: 5 National Forest System land
children.text: The term “National Forest System land” means land of the National Forest System (as defined in ).section 1609(a) of this title

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 6 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Agriculture.

children.heading: Authorized emergency actions to respond to emergency situations
children.label: b Authorized emergency actions to respond to emergency situations

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may make a determination that an emergency situation exists with respect to National Forest System land.

children.heading: Review
children.label: B Review
children.text: An emergency situation determination shall not be subject to objection under the predecisional administrative review processes under part 218 of title 36, Code of Federal Regulations (or successor regulations).

children.heading: Basis of determination
children.label: C Basis of determination
children.text: An emergency situation determination shall be based on an examination of the relevant information.

children.label: A
children.text: the salvage of dead or dying trees;

children.label: B
children.text: the harvest of trees damaged by wind or ice;

children.label: C
children.text: the commercial and noncommercial sanitation harvest of trees to control insects or disease, including trees already infested with insects or disease;

children.label: D
children.text: the reforestation or replanting of fire-impacted areas through planting, control of competing vegetation, or other activities that enhance natural regeneration and restore forest species;

children.label: E
children.text: the removal of hazardous trees in close proximity to roads and trails;

children.label: F
children.text: the removal of hazardous fuels;

children.label: G
children.text: the restoration of water sources or infrastructure;

children.label: H
children.text: the reconstruction of existing utility lines; and

children.label: I
children.text: the replacement of underground cables.

children.heading: Relation to land and resource management plans
children.label: 3 Relation to land and resource management plans
children.text: Any authorized emergency action carried out under paragraph (2) on National Forest System land shall be conducted consistent with the applicable land and resource management plan.

children.heading: Environmental assessment or environmental impact statement
children.label: 1 Environmental assessment or environmental impact statement
A
children.text: the proposed agency action, taking into account the probable environmental consequences of the authorized emergency action and mitigating foreseeable adverse environmental effects, to the extent practicable; and

children.label: B
children.text: the alternative of no action.

children.heading: Public notice
children.label: 2 Public notice
children.text: The Secretary shall provide notice of each authorized emergency action that the Secretary determines requires an environmental assessment or environmental impact statement under paragraph (1), in accordance with applicable regulations and administrative guidelines.

children.heading: Public comment
children.label: 3 Public comment
children.text: The Secretary shall provide an opportunity for public comment during the preparation of any environmental assessment or environmental impact statement under paragraph (1).

children.label: A
children.text: making an emergency situation determination, including a determination that an emergency exists pursuant to section 218.21(a) of title 36, Code of Federal Regulations (or successor regulations); or

children.label: B
children.text: taking an emergency action under section 220.4(b) of title 36, Code of Federal Regulations (or successor regulations).

children.heading: Administrative review of authorized emergency actions
children.label: d Administrative review of authorized emergency actions
children.text: An authorized emergency action carried out under this section shall not be subject to objection under the predecisional administrative review processes established under and section 428 of the Department of the Interior, Environment, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2012 ( note; ).section 6515 of this title16 U.S.C. 6515Public Law 112–74

children.heading: Judicial review of emergency actions
children.label: e Judicial review of emergency actions
children.text: A court shall not enjoin an authorized emergency action under this section if the court determines that the plaintiff is unable to demonstrate that the claim of the plaintiff is likely to succeed on the merits.

children.heading: Notification and guidance
children.label: f Notification and guidance
children.text: The Secretary shall provide notification and guidance to each local field office of the Forest Service to ensure awareness of, compliance with, and appropriate use of the authorized emergency action authority under this section.
